commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can extend the prompt-block stripper and add one regression test on the existing PR surface.; Needs attention: The patch has no supply-chain concern, but the remaining truncated prompt-block gap can still leak source text into spoken output.; Review finding: [P2] Strip truncated prompt echoes before speaking summaries — src/tts/tts-core.ts:81-82 (sha=5006f60c7d5eb9a562cda21f2ade64e4d7567f35)
commands.review_summary:
The PR sanitizes TTS summarization model output before speech synthesis and adds unit plus plugin-contract coverage for prompt/scaffolding cleanup.

PR surface: Source +128, Tests +141. Total +269 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main returns raw joined summary text, and the current PR head still leaves truncated `<text_to_summarize>` echoes unmatched by the new regex. I did not run provider audio playback in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can extend the prompt-block stripper and add one regression test on the existing PR surface.

target.title: fix(tts): sanitize summarized speech text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90364.

TTS summarization now cleans summary-model output before speech synthesis:

- reuses the shared assistant-visible text sanitizer
- removes echoed `<text_to_summarize>` prompt blocks
- strips only prompt-specific leading summarizer preambles while preserving valid first-person and user-request summary prose
- preserves usable summary content after colon/dash prompt preambles
- caps the sanitized summary at the requested speech target length

## Root Cause

`src/tts/tts-core.ts` joined `TextContent` blocks from `completeSimple` and returned that raw string as `summary.summary`. Auto-TTS then assigned that value directly to `textForAudio`, so reasoning tags, model control tokens, prompt echo, or echoed original text could be spoken.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Long-response TTS summaries no longer pass hidden reasoning/control scaffolding, echoed summarization prompt text, or echoed `<text_to_summarize>` content through to the audio text candidate. The review feedback cases are also covered: legitimate first-person summary prose is preserved, valid summaries starting with `The user asked me to summarize ...` are preserved, and usable content after colon-style prompt preambles is preserved.

Real environment tested: Dedicated local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/fix-tts-summary-sanitization` at commit `5006f60c7d`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw source run:

```console
$ pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts
OpenClaw TTS summary proof
summary="Concise audible summary."
outputLength=24
targetLength=120
leakedForbiddenTokens=none
audioText=Concise audible summary.
```

Observed result after fix: The audio text candidate was `Concise audible summary.`. The proof input included summarizer preamble, `<think>` reasoning, `<|assistant|>` control token, tool-call XML, and an echoed `<text_to_summarize>` block; the produced audio text candidate had `leakedForbiddenTokens=none` and stayed under the requested target length.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft/MiniMax audio playback was not run because provider credentials and audio playback setup were not available in this local checkout.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the production `summarizeText` cleanup path from the PR worktree with controlled provider text. It verifies the text handed toward speech synthesis, not the downstream Microsoft audio renderer.

## CI / Review State

- Review comments from ClawSweeper and Codex on earlier heads were addressed through `5006f60c7d` by tightening prompt-echo detection, preserving first-person summary prose, preserving valid `The user asked me to summarize ...` summary prose, and preserving content after colon/dash prompt preambles.
- GitHub workflows are running on the current PR head; current live status is available in the PR Checks tab. Local focused verification below passed on `5006f60c7d`.
- Codex autoreview was attempted before PR creation, but local sandbox escalation for the external Codex review service was rejected, so this PR relies on focused local proof plus GitHub CI/review.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in the dedicated worktree to hydrate dependencies
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/tts-core.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/tts.contract.test.ts packages/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Focused verification passed across 3 Vitest shards: 2 unit-fast files / 165 tests, 2 unit files / 48 tests, and 1 plugin-contract file / 42 tests.
